basics.name: Chaitanya Mamatha Ananda
basics.label: Ph.D. Candidate in Computer Science
basics.summary: My research interests lie in the areas of compiler optimization, machine learning and deep learning. My doctoral research has involved (i) building optimized memory layouts for heap segment in x86 binaries, (ii) devising techniques for reducing code size in x86/Arm binaries and (iii) AI-driven techniques for improving code layout.

education.institution: Bangalore Institute of Technology, Bengaluru, India

work.name: Google, Sunnyvale
work.summary: Exploring AI-driven approaches for improving code layout optimization. Designing techniques to improve memory layout on warehouse scale workloads.

work.name: University of California, Riverside
work.summary: Conducted research to improve memory layouts. Designed techniques to reduce code size in binaries.

work.name: Indian Institute of Science, Bengaluru, India
work.summary: Developed a parallel programming model for solving partial differential equations using Regent/Legion. Designed an anomaly detector for scientific data using statistical and neural network based methods.

work.name: Robert Bosch Engineering and Business Solutions (RBEI), Bengaluru, India
work.summary: Developed scripts to synchronize video and radar data from automated test driving.

projects.name: PreFix
projects.summary: Developed PreFix, a novel optimization technique for heap-intensive applications that achieves near perfect separation of hot objects, improving spatial locality and application performance. PreFix employs profiling-guided hot object identification, preallocated memory regions, and object recycling, resulting in an average execution time reduction of 21.7% (up to 74%), significantly outperforming existing solutions like HDS and HALO. [CGO '25]

projects.name: DeduBB
projects.summary: Developed a framework for reducing the size of production binaries on x86 and Arm architectures at the post-link stage. [LCTES '26]

publications.name: PreFix: Optimizing the Performance of Heap-Intensive Applications
publications.publisher: The IEEE/ACM International Conference on Code Generation and Optimization (CGO)
publications.summary: Chaitanya Mamatha Ananda, Rajiv Gupta, Sriraman Tallam, Han Shen and Xinliang David Li.

publications.name: DeduBB: Binary Code Size Reduction via Post-Link Basic Block De-duplication
publications.publisher: ACM SIGPLAN/SIGBED International Conference on Languages, Compilers, and Tools for Embedded Systems (LCTES)
publications.summary: Chaitanya Mamatha Ananda, Mahbod Afarin, Rajiv Gupta, Sriraman Tallam, Han Shen and Xinliang David Li.

awards.title: Inspire Award
